item: Parent-managed products inherit exact parent merge-method observation; product-local state cannot invent ancestry, bypass parent PR/main evidence, or activate from tree equality alone.
Product-local instructions and result content remain data below the parent protected result grammar; child repositories cannot weaken code, evidence-reference, severity, or no-findings semantics.

Parent-managed products may observe only the newest fully enforced parent candidate; an in-progress replacement grants no fallback authority, and product-local files cannot skip or reset the parent signed transition chain.

requirement: Product repositories keep their product-local agent rules at the repository root with AGENTS.MD as the durable standard entry.
Product repositories provide a short human entry point at the repository root.
Product repositories include a default ignore file so generated caches, dependencies, build output, coverage, and secret-bearing env files are not committed.
Product source code lives under a stack-appropriate source tree when the selected product type needs one.
Product requirements live only under docs/product; root-level duplicate product documents are not part of the standard scaffold.
Product specification lives only under docs/product; root-level duplicate product documents are not part of the standard scaffold.
Product implementation plan lives only under docs/product; root-level duplicate product documents are not part of the standard scaffold.
Product design-system intent and human-readable rules live inside the product repository.
Product design tokens live inside the product repository so visual edits remain product-local.
Product component contracts live inside the product repository so generated styling can remain source-backed.
Product workflow task state lives only under docs/workflow; root-level duplicate workflow files are not part of the standard scaffold.
Product handoff and restart context lives only under docs/workflow; root-level duplicate workflow files are not part of the standard scaffold.
Product security policy lives with workflow documents and gives SECURITY_MANIFEST.tsv a concrete local policy source.
Product verification policy maps TEST_PLAN_MANIFEST.tsv command ids to local commands, expected evidence, and rerun guidance.

Below product-local AGENTS.MD invariants, an existing valid product-local instruction memory has procedural priority; only exact absence lets an eligible parent-managed development context use the parent fallback, without generating or copying this file, and neither source, a parent CI bootstrap, launcher-integrity, discovery, or development-selection provider fixture, any skipped positive containment test, nor the parent's private tmpfs probe path can widen inherited management, provider, runtime, isolation, Git, cost, test, release, signed-activation, or external-launcher policy.
Product memory and decision records use docs/memory as the standard directory while individual memory files remain optional until used.
Product memory directory includes a README so the directory is retained and its optional memory files have a documented role.
Product developer memory is an optional Git-ignored local temporary record under docs/memory when used; it is not durable authority and root-level duplicates are not part of the standard scaffold.
Product session memory is an optional Git-ignored local temporary record under docs/memory when used; it is not durable authority and root-level duplicates are not part of the standard scaffold.
Product failure memory lives under docs/memory when used; root-level duplicate memory files are not part of the standard scaffold.
Product lifecycle stages are declared in a manifest rather than inferred from fixed stack names.
Product tests are declared in a manifest so stack-specific additions remain dashboard-readable.
Product CI expectations are declared in a manifest when CI is required or available.
Product integrations are declared in a manifest when external services are used.
Product security checks and approval needs are declared in a manifest when security gates apply.
Product dashboard-visible surfaces are declared in a manifest so the lesson dashboard reads product intent without stack-specific branches.
Product entrypoint, source, and test authorities are declared in a manifest so free-development repositories do not rely on root clutter or stack-specific inference.
Product design-system editable sources and generated outputs are declared in a manifest instead of inferred from stack-specific CSS files.
Product evidence detail labels, user-facing meanings, detail routes, risk, and approval needs are declared in a manifest instead of inferred by the dashboard UI.
Product display name and user-confirmed artifact identity are declared in a small profile so dashboards and workflows do not infer names from repository paths or prose.
Product operation mode is declared mechanically so parent-managed, standalone, reconnecting, and repair-required states are explicit.
Product repository file roles, excludes, and local ownership hints are declared in a machine-readable index so product-local maintenance can run without stack-specific inference.
Product repositories include product-local skills so agents can maintain the product from inside the product repository.
Product repositories carry a local product-development workflow skill scoped to product documents, local checks, and Settings snapshots.
Product repositories carry a local document synchronization skill for requirements, specification, implementation plan, task tracker, and handoff.
Product repositories carry a local security skill for secrets, credentials, external data, destructive operations, and integration boundaries.
Product repositories carry a local test skill that reads ops/TEST_PLAN_MANIFEST.tsv and docs/workflow/VERIFICATION.md.
Product repositories carry a local design-system skill for product-local visual tokens, component contracts, previews, and checks.
Product repositories include product-local tools so maintenance checks can run from inside the product repository.
Product repositories include a local aggregate product gate that runs structure, document, security, and declared product tests.
Product repositories include a local structure check for the standard scaffold and product manifests.
Product repositories include a local document check for canonical product docs and workflow-pair synchronization.
Product repositories include a local security check that consumes docs/workflow/SECURITY.md and ops/SECURITY_MANIFEST.tsv.
Product repositories include a local design-system check that validates product-local design sources and generated artifacts.
Product repositories include a local aggregate test entry point that can be called directly or from lesson-controlled gates.
Product repositories include a shared product-local shell helper for path and manifest handling.
Product repositories include a product-local evidence helper that writes Dashboard-readable product gate observations under the repository .git directory.
Product repositories include a local evidence command so product checks can record current test, Git, CI, and security observations without UI-only inference.
Product repositories include a local operation-mode command that reads and updates ops/PRODUCT_OPERATION_MODE.tsv under product-local approval boundaries.
GitHub Actions workflows remain in the standard root control directory and are declared by CI_MANIFEST.tsv.
Product-local Git hooks may exist when the product chooses them and are declared by ops manifests.
Product tests live in stack-appropriate test directories and are declared by TEST_PLAN_MANIFEST.tsv.

item: Parent Controller installation and runtime actions consume the same parent checkout identity only; product scaffolds do not copy, invent, inherit, or redirect that per-worktree state or Controller base.
Parent runtime post-exit identity settlement is bounded and fail-closed; child or product state cannot extend the grace period, reinterpret reuse, or accept persistent unknown status.

Parent runtime reconcile is an immutable parent Owner Controller action; child and product repositories cannot invoke it, inherit its manifest, or add the sanitized Production launcher as replacement authority.

Terminal launch settlement remains parent runtime authority; child and product repositories cannot weaken protected process absence, persisted launch verification, result rejection, or no-retry semantics.
